chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.S-327 of 2020 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For hearing of main case. 04.11.2022 Mr. Wali Muhammad Khoso, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Allah Bachayo Soomro, Additional Advocate General, Sindh. Mir Shakir Ali Talpur, Advocate has filed power on behalf of respondent No.4 same is taken on record. == Through instant constitutional petition, the petitioner has prayed the following relief as under: “A. To set aside the impugned order dated 13.07.2020 passed by the learned 3rd Additional Sessions Judge & Ex-Officio Justice of Peace Hyderabad and remand back the case to the learned trial court with directions to pass appropriate order afresh on Contempt Application in Cr.Misc.Application No.832 of 2020 filed by the petitioner and give weight to the statement dated 08.07.2020 (annexure “D”), FIR No.103 of 2020 of Police Station Husri, Section 166 PPC 1860, call CDR from the respondent No.2 and afford full opportunity of hearing to the petitioner. B. Costs of the petition may be saddled upon the respondents. C. Any other relief(s) which this Honourable Court deems fit, just and proper in foavur of the petitioner. Learned counsel for petitioner submits that private respondents are causing harassment to the petitioner and his family members on the behest of criminal litigation pending between them before Vth Additional Sessions Judge Hyderabad; however, his assertion has been controverted by learned counsel appearing for respondent No.4. During course of arguments parties agreed for disposal of instant matter by submitting that if directions are issued to police that no harassment shall be caused to either party and police shall be neutral in their private affairs.

Learned counsel for petitioner submits that private respondents are causing harassment to the petitioner and his family members on the behest of criminal litigation pending between them before Vth Additional Sessions Judge Hyderabad; however, his assertion has been controverted by learned counsel appearing for respondent No.4. During course of arguments parties agreed for disposal of instant matter by submitting that if directions are issued to police that no harassment shall be caused to either party and police shall be neutral in their private affairs. Learned Additional Advocate General, Sindh denied the allegations and stated that official respondents will act strictly in accordance with law and no harassment will be caused to either party and they will not interfere in their criminal dispute provided that law permits them. In view of above statement of learned Additional Advocate General, Sindh instant petition stands disposed of. However, it is made clear that every action of the official respondents shall be subject to law and police shall not act at the behest of any influential person; and shall be neutral in private affairs pending between the parties including criminal litigation if any. JUDGE Muhammad Danish
